mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so a persistent monthly decline predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in a majority of months with a falling FEDFUNDS trend, or if the rolling 3-year correlation between FEDFUNDS changes and forward returns is persistently near zero.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in RSAFS above its recent baseline signals stronger consumer demand and revenue growth, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if RSAFS breakouts above the 6-month moving average are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time over a full sample or any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in UNRATE signals worsening labor market slack and higher recession odds, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment shocks of at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months are not followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more than half the time, or if the signal flips positive in a stable way across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO and PAYEMS both strengthen together, it indicates synchronized production and payroll expansion that supports earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint improvements in INDPRO and PAYEMS do not outperform either series alone for predicting positive 20-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not significant in rolling 3-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate above neutral tightens financial conditions and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns significantly more often than the unconditional baseline, or if the sign becomes positive in tightening windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE combined with weak PAYEMS growth indicates labor market deterioration and imminent earnings downgrades, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint state of rising UNRATE and slowing PAYEMS does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns better than UNRATE alone, or if positive returns dominate in triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST below its recent trend marks demand weakening and a coming slowdown in household-related activity, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average by more than 10%
failure_condition: Reject if housing starts breaks below trend fail to precede negative 60-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the predictive effect vanishes in rolling 5-year samples.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, rising INDPRO together with improving PAYEMS and stable unemployment indicates normalization of supply and labor conditions, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined recovery state does not produce positive 60-day SPX returns in a majority of episodes, or if the interaction loses significance once any single proxy is removed.
